MD. SHOAIB SIKDER 
Software Engineering Student 
 
 01777936837                         github.com/ShoaibSikder                     
 shoaibsikder0@gmail.com     linkedin.com/in/md-shoaib-sikder 
 
 
EDUCATION  
 
 
Daffodil International University 
BSc. in Software Engineering 
2023 - Present 
 
Bangladesh Gas Fields School & 
College 
Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) 
2021 
 
Sohagpur A.S.A.M School 
Secondary School Certificate (SSC) 
2019 
 
 
SKILLS  
 
 
Programming Languages 
 
 
Frontend 
 
    
Backend 
 
Databases 
 
 
Tools & Others 
 
 
LANGUAGES  
 
 
• Bangla 
• English 
• Hindi 
ABOUT  ME 
 
Hi! I'm MD. Shoaib Sikder, a Software Engineering student who enjoys 
turning ideas into polished digital experiences. I like working across full 
stack development, backend logic, and practical software that people 
can actually use. I care  about clean UI, readable code, practical problem 
solving, and steady growth across frontend and backend development. 
 
EXPERIENCE  
 
 
Intern (Preparation Phase) Ongoing 
Self-Directed 
Actively preparing for internship opportunities by building projects, 
learning industry practices, and improving coding skills to be internship-
ready. 
 
PROJECT  EXPERIENCE  
 
BachelorsNest  GitHub 
FULL STACK - FLAT RENTAL PLATFORM 
A full stack bachelor flat rental platform built to help users discover, 
manage, and rent bachelor-friendly accommodations through a modern 
web-based system with secure authentication. 
Python, Django, React, SQLite, REST API, JWT Authentication 
 
BackgroundRemover  GitHub 
AI TOOL - IMAGE PROCESSING 
An AI-powered image processing application that automatically removes 
image backgrounds using API-driven background segmentation and a 
clean responsive frontend interface. 
React, Vite, JavaScript, TailwindCSS, AI Integration, REST API 
 
Note Sharing System  GitHub 
ACADEMIC - FILE SHARING SYSTEM 
A collaborative academic note sharing platform where students can upload, 
access, and manage study materials efficiently through authentication. 
PHP, MySQL, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, File Upload System 
 
Phishing URL Detector  GitHub 
CYBERSECURITY - MACHINE LEARNING 
A machine learning based cybersecurity project that detects phishing URLs 
by analyzing malicious link patterns and classifying suspicious websites. 
Python, Machine Learning, Scikit-learn, Pandas, Data Analysis 
 
Health Monitoring System  GitHub 
C PROGRAMMING - HEALTHCARE SYSTEM 
A healthcare management application developed in C that analyzes 
symptoms, identifies diseases, and generates prescriptions through 
structured logic. 
C Programming, Data Structures, Algorithms, System Logic 
 
Todo List  GitHub 
PRODUCTIVITY - TASK MANAGEMENT 
A responsive task management application developed to organize daily 
activities with dynamic task updates, local data persistence, and interactive 
behavior. 
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, DOM Manipulation, Local Storage 
 
Responsive Portfolio  GitHub 
PORTFOLIO - RESPONSIVE WEB DESIGN 
A fully responsive personal portfolio website developed to showcase 
projects, education, and skills with smooth scrolling and interactive 
navigation. 
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Responsive Design, Mobile First 
 
 
REFERENCES  
 
 
 
Mr. Fahim Faisal 
Lecturer (DIU) 
Phone: 01778527920 
Email: faisal.cse505.c@diu.edu.bd 
Mr. Z N M Zarif Mahmud 
Lecturer (DIU) 
Phone: 01740087267 
Email: zarif.cse0538.c@diu.edu.bd 
HTML 
 CSS 
 React 
 Tailwind CSS

MD. SHOAIB SIKDER 
Software Engineering Student 
 
 01777936837                         github.com/ShoaibSikder                     
 shoaibsikder0@gmail.com     linkedin.com/in/md-shoaib-sikder 
 
 
EDUCATION  
 
 
Daffodil International University 
BSc. in Software Engineering 
2023 - Present 
 
Bangladesh Gas Fields School & 
College 
Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) 
2021 
 
Sohagpur A.S.A.M School 
Secondary School Certificate (SSC) 
2019 
 
 
SKILLS  
 
 
Programming Languages 
 
 
Frontend 
 
    
Backend 
 
Databases 
 
 
Tools & Others 
 
 
LANGUAGES  
 
 
• Bangla 
• English 
• Hindi 
ABOUT  ME 
 
Hi! I'm MD. Shoaib Sikder, a Software Engineering student who enjoys 
turning ideas into polished digital experiences. I like working across full 
stack development, backend logic, and practical software that people 
can actually use. I care  about clean UI, readable code, practical problem 
solving, and steady growth across frontend and backend development. 
 
EXPERIENCE  
 
 
Intern (Preparation Phase) Ongoing 
Self-Directed 
Actively preparing for internship opportunities by building projects, 
learning industry practices, and improving coding skills to be internship-
ready. 
 
PROJECT  EXPERIENCE  
 
BachelorsNest  GitHub 
FULL STACK - FLAT RENTAL PLATFORM 
A full stack bachelor flat rental platform built to help users discover, 
manage, and rent bachelor-friendly accommodations through a modern 
web-based system with secure authentication. 
Python, Django, React, SQLite, REST API, JWT Authentication 
 
BackgroundRemover  GitHub 
AI TOOL - IMAGE PROCESSING 
An AI-powered image processing application that automatically removes 
image backgrounds using API-driven background segmentation and a 
clean responsive frontend interface. 
React, Vite, JavaScript, TailwindCSS, AI Integration, REST API 
 
Note Sharing System  GitHub 
ACADEMIC - FILE SHARING SYSTEM 
A collaborative academic note sharing platform where students can upload, 
access, and manage study materials efficiently through authentication. 
PHP, MySQL, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, File Upload System 
 
Phishing URL Detector  GitHub 
CYBERSECURITY - MACHINE LEARNING 
A machine learning based cybersecurity project that detects phishing URLs 
by analyzing malicious link patterns and classifying suspicious websites. 
Python, Machine Learning, Scikit-learn, Pandas, Data Analysis 
 
Health Monitoring System  GitHub 
C PROGRAMMING - HEALTHCARE SYSTEM 
A healthcare management application developed in C that analyzes 
symptoms, identifies diseases, and generates prescriptions through 
structured logic. 
C Programming, Data Structures, Algorithms, System Logic 
 
Todo List  GitHub 
PRODUCTIVITY - TASK MANAGEMENT 
A responsive task management application developed to organize daily 
activities with dynamic task updates, local data persistence, and interactive 
behavior. 
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, DOM Manipulation, Local Storage 
 
Responsive Portfolio  GitHub 
PORTFOLIO - RESPONSIVE WEB DESIGN 
A fully responsive personal portfolio website developed to showcase 
projects, education, and skills with smooth scrolling and interactive 
navigation. 
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Responsive Design, Mobile First 
 
 
REFERENCES  
 
 
 
Mr. Fahim Faisal 
Lecturer (DIU) 
Phone: 01778527920 
Email: faisal.cse505.c@diu.edu.bd 
Mr. Z N M Zarif Mahmud 
Lecturer (DIU) 
Phone: 01740087267 
Email: zarif.cse0538.c@diu.edu.bd 
HTML 
 CSS 
 React 
 Tailwind CSS

ABOUT  ME 
 
Hi! I'm MD. Shoaib Sikder, a Software Engineering student who enjoys 
turning ideas into polished digital experiences. I like working across full 
stack development, backend logic, and practical software that people 
can actually use. I care  about clean UI, readable code, practical problem 
solving, and steady growth across frontend and backend development.

learning industry practices, and improving coding skills to be internship-
ready.

fields.title: RM_Lec_13_14_Research Proposal
fields.extracted_text:
Research Proposal SWE 686 Research Design and Applications for Data and Analysis 13-14 7 Summer 2025 Dr. S M Hasan Mahmud Associate Professor, Department of Software Engineering drhasan.swe@diu.edu.bd

Generalized Form of a RP 7/30/2025 10

Project Title 7/30/2025 11 The title of a research project should be clear and specific. More than ten words are recommended for the title. Acronyms should not be used as the assessment panel might not be familiar with their use The title should indirectly indicate the science/engineering discipline of the research so that the readers can direct the project proposal to those who have relevant experience in the discipline.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 12 While the project outline contains technical detail, the project team needs to convince the project assessment team that the project is worthy of funding. Often the assessment team consists of generalists who do not have specific detailed knowledge of the research topic and the analysis methods proposed in the project outline. Provide a short, clear statement about how the project will benefit their organization and how it fits into the goals and objectives of the research field.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 13 Example: An antenna team aims to design new antennas that will improve the range of small wireless sensors used in a nursing home. The assessment team from the research granting authority might want to know the current and intended application of the wireless sensors and how an increased range will improve the lives of aged people living independently. The project summary should also contain keywords so that the literature in the field can be thoroughly surveyed for competing technologies and research outcomes.

Background 7/30/2025 14 Background information should justify the necessity of your project . Do preliminary studies indicate that your project is necessary and feasible? Literature review: Include title of citation, authors, date, journal and page number Check the citations for accuracy

Background… 7/30/2025 15 Start with a very broad perspective of the main subject area, before gradually narrowing the focus to the central problem under investigation. In doing so, cover the following aspects of your study area: an overview of the main area under study; a historical perspective (development, growth, etc.) pertinent to the study area; philosophical or ideological issues relating to the topic; trends in terms of prevalence, if appropriate; major theories, if any; the main issues, problems and advances in the subject area under study; important theoretical and practical issues relating to the central problem under study; the main findings relating to the core issue(s).

Background… 7/30/2025 16 A presentation of any pertinent data obtained in the investigator’s lab (if any) Displayed in an organized fashion (tables, graphs and other figures) Illustrates the skills and techniques available to the investigator Includes a section of recent publications or manuscripts by the investigator Sec: F /G/I

Problem Statement 7/30/2025 17 Some of the main research questions that you would like to answer through your study should also be raised, and a rationale and relevance for each should be provided. Specifically, this section should: identify the issues that are the basis of your study; specify the various aspects of/perspectives on these issues; identify the main gaps in the existing body of knowledge; raise some of the main research questions that you want to answer through your study; identify what knowledge is available concerning your questions, specifying the differences of opinion in the literature regarding these questions if differences exist; develop a rationale for your study with particular reference to how your study will fill the identified gaps.

Objectives of the Study 7/30/2025 18 The objectives of the study should be clearly stated and specific in nature. Each sub-objective should delineate only one issue. Use action-oriented verbs such as ‘to determine’, ‘to investigate’ and ‘to analyse’ in formulating sub-objectives, which should be numerically listed.

Objectives of the Study: Example 7/30/2025 19 Problem Statement: When will a MEMs based silicon micro-switch fail if it is exposed to the atmosphere? Objectives: To measure the surface contamination of silicon exposed to the atmosphere; To measure the mechanical properties of a silicon beam when subjected to continued use; To model the experimentally derived data using 3D multi-physics .

Content Introduction Research proposal content Project title, project summary and Background Ethical issues Research plan, methodology, timing Budget and justification Research team Research timeline 7/30/2025 2

Measurement Procedures 7/30/2025 20 Justify your choice of research tool, highlighting its strengths and pointing out its weaknesses. Then outline the major segments of your research tool and their relevance to the main objectives of the study. If you are using a standard instrument, briefly discuss the availability of evidence on its reliability and validity. If you adapt or modify it in any way, describe and explain the changes you have made. Ideally, for quantitative studies you should attach a copy of the research instrument to your proposal.

Ethical Issues 7/30/2025 21 All academic institutions are particular about any ethical issues that research may have. To deal with them, all institutions have some form of policy on ethics. You need to be acquainted with your institution’s policy. It is imperative that in your proposal you identify any ethical issues and describe how you propose to deal with them

Problems and Limitations This section should list any problems you think you might encounter concerning, for example, the availability of data, securing permission from the agency/organisation to carry out the study, obtaining the sample, or any other aspect of the study. Problems refer to difficulties relating to logistical details. It is important to be aware of – and communicate – any limitations that could affect the validity of your conclusions and generalisations. 7/30/2025 22

Research Plan, Methodology & Timing Experimental design Innovations Limitations / anticipated difficulties Alternative approaches Time sequence 7/30/2025 23

Research Plan ... Organized sequentially by specific aims stated A detailed description of proposed experiments including: Rationale Design Anticipated results and Alternative strategies Should be planned to methodically address the specific questions stated under specific aims 7/30/2025 24

Analysis of Data In general terms, describe the strategy you intend to use for data analysis. Specify whether the data will be analysed manually or by computer. For computer analysis, identify the program and where appropriate the statistical procedures you plan to perform on the data. For quantitative studies also identify the main variables for cross-tabulation. You need to specify which particular strategy you are proposing for data analysis for your study . 7/30/2025 25

Significance, Outcomes & Innovation Show how the results of your research will fill identified gaps in existing knowledge. Will the results of your research have benefits the country and/or the funding agency? Provide a list of deliverables which will arise from the project. Deliverables are things which can be accessed by others such as reports, software code, presentations, images, etc. Reports, presentations, patents, designs, objects, etc. can be delivered to the granting authority as an indication of progress in the research project. New knowledge is only created when it is publicly available. In some cases, the research team might specify the conferences and journals where their results will be disseminated. 7/30/2025 26

budget 7/30/2025 27 The budget is a very important aspect of the research proposal. The research plan must be a cost effective strategy for the interested partners. Don’t inflate figures An inflated, unjustified budget is likely to result in no funding or reduced funding of the project. Make accurate calculations Direct Costs can be specifically documented, eg : salaries; operating expenses (printing, consumables); travel (mileage, fares, lodging, sustenance); equipment; etc. ..

Budget… Indirect Costs real costs that cannot be easily identified as specific to a particular project, eg : utilities; maintenance of space and equipment; security; computer services; legal services; accounting services; payroll services. 7/30/2025 28

Budget Justification Justify every item. Do not merely restate proposed expenditure. The justification of the budget must match the research methods and analysis outlined in the research proposal. Explain why the project could not proceed/would not be successful without these items 7/30/2025 29

Objectives To understand how to write a research  proposal To understand the structure of research  proposal To understand how to cite literatures To understand how to write citation and  reference 7/30/2025 3

7/30/2025 30

Conflict of Interest When a research team receives funding from a commercial partner, some ethical issues might arise In particular, researchers must ensure that the results are presented in an unbiased manner and that all conflicts of interest are clearly stated in any publications, presentations and reports This can be done in the acknowledgements section of the published paper 7/30/2025 31

Research Team It is rare for a project to involve only one researcher. The project leader (often called the Chief Investigator or Principal Investigator) should take the lead in approaching people who will make a substantial contribution to the project. The project leader is the person who will manage the project in all respects, who will be responsible for the expenditure of the budget, ensure that the milestones are met and the project outcomes are delivered. 7/30/2025 32

Research Team… In higher education for a PhD thesis, the project leader is usually the research student, however, the thesis topic is commonly initiated by the primary academic supervisor. –Commonly students do not control the expenditure of research funds. This is usually the responsibility of the primary supervisor. If the project contains many co-researchers and collaborators: Explain the roles of each participant in the project. Include MoU/ MoA /LOI in the proposal when collaborating with other institution/industry 7/30/2025 33

Research Schedule You must set yourself dates as you need to complete the research within a certain time-frame. List the various operational steps you need to undertake and indicate against each the date by which you aim to complete that task. Remember to keep some time towards the end as a ‘cushion’ for unforeseen circumstances. In a research project, there is a long and unexpected delay in the delivery of a test instrument. Develop a chart. 7/30/2025 34

Gantt Chart 7/30/2025 35

Research Timelines Simple representation of timelines for a project. The crosses represent major time requirements. Q refers to one quarter of one year. Only the major deliverables  should be included. The work titles must be specific (not generic as presented in this illustration). 7/30/2025 36

Research Timelines … 7/30/2025 37

Appendix Include relevant publications/ manuscripts in preparation from your lab Any detailed experimental protocols/methods that are too long to include in the grant In the case of quantitative studies, attach your research instrument. Attach flowchart of your research activities 7/30/2025 38

Reasons for Rejection Absence of innovative ideas or hypothesis Will provide only an incremental advance Not exciting or cutting edge Errors Unclear or incomplete expression of aims Faulty logic or experimental design Less than rigorous presentation 7/30/2025 39

Where we are? 7/30/2025 4

Reasons for Rejection Unrealistic, sloppy or incomplete Resources and facilities not in place PI qualifications/expertise not evident Necessary collaborations not documented 7/30/2025 40

COTENTS OF RESEARCH PROPOSAL 7/30/2025 41

What is Research Proposal? After you have ideas, then you can state the  general problems or general issues of your  “research” interest Elaborate the problems by reviewing  literatures Make sure the problems are worth of research See previous course‐material: how to review  literatures Design steps to solve the problems‐‐ methodology Use all related theories, methods, techniques,  processes, procedures, tools, etc., found in the 7/30/2025 5

RESEARCH PROPOSAL FLOW CHART 7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 6

Introduction A research proposal is an overall plan, scheme, structure and strategy designed to obtain answer to the research question Ensures the reviewers of the validity of the methodology for obtaining answers to your research questions accurately and objectively. Explain to your research supervisor and reviewers the following information about your study : what you are proposing to do; how you plan to find answers to what you are proposing; why you selected the proposed strategies of investigation 7/30/2025 7

7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 8

Research Proposal Content A research proposal should contain the following information about your study: an introduction, including a brief literature review, theoretical framework that underpins your study objectives or research questions of your study hypotheses to be tested, if applicable study design that you are proposing to adopt setting for your study research instrument(s) you are planning to use ethical issues involved and how you propose to deal with them data processing procedures problems and limitations of the study proposed time-frame for the project 7/30/2025 9

For quantitative studies also identify the main variables for cross-tabulation. You need to specify which particular strategy you are proposing for data analysis for your study . 7/30/2025 25

Significance, Outcomes & Innovation Show how the results of your research will fill identified gaps in existing knowledge. Will the results of your research have benefits the country and/or the funding agency? Provide a list of deliverables which will arise from the project. Deliverables are things which can be accessed by others such as reports, software code, presentations, images, etc. Reports, presentations, patents, designs, objects, etc. can be delivered to the granting authority as an indication of progress in the research project. New knowledge is only created when it is publicly available. In some cases, the research team might specify the conferences and journals where their results will be disseminated. 7/30/2025 26

budget 7/30/2025 27 The budget is a very important aspect of the research proposal. The research plan must be a cost effective strategy for the interested partners. Don’t inflate figures An inflated, unjustified budget is likely to result in no funding or reduced funding of the project. Make accurate calculations Direct Costs can be specifically documented, eg : salaries; operating expenses (printing, consumables); travel (mileage, fares, lodging, sustenance); equipment; etc. ..

Budget… Indirect Costs real costs that cannot be easily identified as specific to a particular project, eg : utilities; maintenance of space and equipment; security; computer services; legal services; accounting services; payroll services. 7/30/2025 28

Budget Justification Justify every item. Do not merely restate proposed expenditure. The justification of the budget must match the research methods and analysis outlined in the research proposal. Explain why the project could not proceed/would not be successful without these items 7/30/2025 29

Objectives To understand how to write a research  proposal To understand the structure of research  proposal To understand how to cite literatures To understand how to write citation and  reference 7/30/2025 3

7/30/2025 30

Conflict of Interest When a research team receives funding from a commercial partner, some ethical issues might arise In particular, researchers must ensure that the results are presented in an unbiased manner and that all conflicts of interest are clearly stated in any publications, presentations and reports This can be done in the acknowledgements section of the published paper 7/30/2025 31

Research Team It is rare for a project to involve only one researcher. The project leader (often called the Chief Investigator or Principal Investigator) should take the lead in approaching people who will make a substantial contribution to the project. The project leader is the person who will manage the project in all respects, who will be responsible for the expenditure of the budget, ensure that the milestones are met and the project outcomes are delivered. 7/30/2025 32

Research Team… In higher education for a PhD thesis, the project leader is usually the research student, however, the thesis topic is commonly initiated by the primary academic supervisor. –Commonly students do not control the expenditure of research funds. This is usually the responsibility of the primary supervisor. If the project contains many co-researchers and collaborators: Explain the roles of each participant in the project. Include MoU/ MoA /LOI in the proposal when collaborating with other institution/industry 7/30/2025 33

Research Schedule You must set yourself dates as you need to complete the research within a certain time-frame. List the various operational steps you need to undertake and indicate against each the date by which you aim to complete that task. Remember to keep some time towards the end as a ‘cushion’ for unforeseen circumstances. In a research project, there is a long and unexpected delay in the delivery of a test instrument. Develop a chart. 7/30/2025 34

Gantt Chart 7/30/2025 35

Research Timelines Simple representation of timelines for a project. The crosses represent major time requirements. Q refers to one quarter of one year. Only the major deliverables  should be included. The work titles must be specific (not generic as presented in this illustration). 7/30/2025 36

Research Timelines … 7/30/2025 37

Appendix Include relevant publications/ manuscripts in preparation from your lab Any detailed experimental protocols/methods that are too long to include in the grant In the case of quantitative studies, attach your research instrument. Attach flowchart of your research activities 7/30/2025 38

Reasons for Rejection Absence of innovative ideas or hypothesis Will provide only an incremental advance Not exciting or cutting edge Errors Unclear or incomplete expression of aims Faulty logic or experimental design Less than rigorous presentation 7/30/2025 39

Where we are? 7/30/2025 4

Reasons for Rejection Unrealistic, sloppy or incomplete Resources and facilities not in place PI qualifications/expertise not evident Necessary collaborations not documented 7/30/2025 40

COTENTS OF RESEARCH PROPOSAL 7/30/2025 41

What is Research Proposal? After you have ideas, then you can state the  general problems or general issues of your  “research” interest Elaborate the problems by reviewing  literatures Make sure the problems are worth of research See previous course‐material: how to review  literatures Design steps to solve the problems‐‐ methodology Use all related theories, methods, techniques,  processes, procedures, tools, etc., found in the 7/30/2025 5

RESEARCH PROPOSAL FLOW CHART 7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 6

Introduction A research proposal is an overall plan, scheme, structure and strategy designed to obtain answer to the research question Ensures the reviewers of the validity of the methodology for obtaining answers to your research questions accurately and objectively. Explain to your research supervisor and reviewers the following information about your study : what you are proposing to do; how you plan to find answers to what you are proposing; why you selected the proposed strategies of investigation 7/30/2025 7

7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 8

Research Proposal Content A research proposal should contain the following information about your study: an introduction, including a brief literature review, theoretical framework that underpins your study objectives or research questions of your study hypotheses to be tested, if applicable study design that you are proposing to adopt setting for your study research instrument(s) you are planning to use ethical issues involved and how you propose to deal with them data processing procedures problems and limitations of the study proposed time-frame for the project 7/30/2025 9

fields.title: RM_Lec 15_16 Thesis Writing
fields.extracted_text:
Thesis Writing SWE 686 Research Design and Applications for Data and Analysis 15-16 8 Summer 2025 Dr. S M Hasan Mahmud Associate Professor, Department of Software Engineering drhasan.swe@diu.edu.bd

Structure of a Thesis 7/30/2025 10 Introduction/Theme Why am I doing this? Literature Review What is known? What is unknown? Aims What do/did I hope to find out? Methodology How is/was it found out? Results What di d I find? Discussion What does it mean? Conclusion What is the significance? What applications? What to next?

Planning the Thesis Fieldwork, literature review, Interviews; Methodology (1 months) Proof of Principle (2 months) Data analysis and validation Maintain notes (laboratory workbooks) [also keep notes on thoughts from the start] A thesis takes about 180 mins a day 7/30/2025 11

How long it takes to write thesis? Longer than you think!! It is wise to allow at least one complete term for writing the thesis Writing the thesis requires the complete organization of your arguments and results Fixing those weaknesses that takes time Supervisors are sometimes not quick at reviewing and returning drafts Bottom line: leave yourself enough time. A rush job has painful consequences at the defence. 7/30/2025 12

Developing the Thesis Convert plan/argument into chapters at least one chapter per sentence perhaps more than one, sometimes Develop a physical “structure” set up folders for each chapter have a plan for each chapter Allow the plan to evolve don’t be worried about changing new results may require reinterpretations 7/30/2025 14

Simple Structure of a Thesis The Bellman’s Rule of Three Overall Introduction – what the thesis says; context of the hypothesis Body of the thesis – what makes the thesis Conclusion – what the thesis said; what it means for future directions Each chapter Introduction – what this chapter says; where it sits in the theme Body of the chapter – methods, results Conclusion – what this chapter means; how it leads logically to the next 7/30/2025 15

Simple Structure of a Thesis Each paragraph Introduction – broaches an idea; links to previous ideas Body of the paragraph – describes the idea Conclusion – concludes the idea (and links to next) *Not just repetition – linking, theme, rationale, integration 7/30/2025 16

Nested Hourglass Model 7/30/2025 17 The whole thesis Each section, subsection Most Paragraphs Broad focus at the beginning and end Specifics/narrow focus in the middle

Abstract Write this LAST! Abstracts should not exceed 500 words Should be self-contained Written to attract readers to your article or thesis, gives a good initial impression Summary of the contents of the thesis Brief but contains sufficient detail motivation for the work (problem statement) project objectives techniques employed main results and conclusions 7/30/2025 18

Thesis Content: Preface Acknowledge Table of Contents List of Figures List of Tables List of Appendices List of Symbols and Abbreviation SEC: K 7/30/2025 19

What is a Thesis? A thesis is an original contribution to knowledge It describes the complete process of the project starting from the problem formulation to the solution and conclusions in a Scientific and Methodical manner. 7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 2

Introduction Write this second to last! You probably wrote this for your thesis proposal; REWRITE IT AFTER body of thesis is written This is a general introduction to what the thesis is all about. Briefly summarize the question, and some of the reasons why it is a worthwhile question The aims and objectives of the study The scope or outline of the research approach A description of the contents of each section 7/30/2025 20

Background A brief section giving background information may be necessary. Your readers may not have any experience with some of the material needed to follow your thesis, so you need to give it to them. A more informative title is usually better. 7/30/2025 21

Literature Review Provides context for and details about the motivation for the project States why the problem is important Sets the scene for the work described in the thesis Describes what others have done and hence sets a benchmark for the current project Justifies the use of specific techniques or problem solving procedures 7/30/2025 22

Review: State-of-the-Art Limited to the state of the art relevant to your thesis. Again, a specific heading is appropriate The idea is to present (not analyze ) the major ideas in the state of the art right up to, but not including, your own personal brilliant ideas. You organize this section by idea, and not by author or by publication. Do not simply quote or paraphrase the contents of published articles. Weave the information into focused views. Demonstrate your deeper understanding of the topic . Do not be tempted to summarize everything you have read; only include those relevant to your main points 7/30/2025 23

Research Question or Problem Statement A concise statement of the question that your thesis tackles Justification, by direct reference to previous work, that your question is previously unanswered. This is where you analyze the information which you presented in the “state of the art” section Discussion of why it is worthwhile to answer this question. 7/30/2025 24

Data & Interpretation No standard form. But still organized! One or several sections and subsections. Methods, Data, Interpretation sections are separate Only one purpose: to convince the advisor (reader/reviewer) that you answered the question or solved the problem stated in the previous section. Present data that is relevant to answering the question or solving the problem: if there were blind alleys and dead ends, do not include these, unless specifically relevant to the demonstration that you answered the thesis question . Note for some theses it may be important to include these in an appendix 7/30/2025 25

Research Methods Depending on your topic this may be one paragraph or a long section If measurement error is important to your study, state how this was assessed. 7/30/2025 26

Data Presentation Draft your figures first: (A picture is worth a thousand words) Make captions stand alone Use enough figures to present the data that justifies your interpretations and conclusions. No more, no less. (Don’t use 1000 words when 500 will do) Write your text around your figures 7/30/2025 27

Interpretation Keep separate from data, clearly distinguished by paragraph, section, and/or words like “are interpreted to show”. Depending on your topic, it is often useful to subdivide interpretation into a “local” or small scale (directly flows from your data) and a “regional” or “big picture” scale, that flows from consideration of your data with that of others. This latter type is usually included in the “discussion” section. 7/30/2025 28

Discussion Look at discussion sections in papers in your field. See what they cover. Usually is a broader scale interpretation than just your data (relate to previous published results) Addresses the bigger problems of your research topic and how your study fits into solving those problems Is NOT a conclusion section 7/30/2025 29

Thesis ... Exposition of original research in context Constitutes objective evidence of the author’s knowledge and capabilities in the field of interest and is therefore a fair means to gauge them Your thesis must show 2 (two) important things: you have identified a worthwhile problem or question which has not been previously answered you have solved the problem or answered the question. 7/30/2025 3

Conclusions Summary of Contributions Future Research Conclusions are not a rambling summary of the thesis: they are short, concise statements of the inferences that you have made because of your work. Organize conclusions as short numbered paragraphs, ordered from most to least important. All conclusions should be directly related to the research question stated 7/30/2025 30

Use Proper Tools Spreadsheets, analysis tools Plotting programs Graphics programs ENDNOTE/ RefMan /Mendeley/ ProCite / LaTeX Writing resources Start learning these before you collect the data Use turnitin.com to check the originality of text 7/30/2025 31

References All references cited, including those in Tables and Figure captions. No more, no less. The references should be written consistently in the American Psychological Association (APA) or IEEE or any other format Each reference should be written in single spacing format and a double space should be left between references. Use ENDNOTE or any other program (start NOW building your library database) 7/30/2025 32

References… APA format: http://www.bibme.org/citation-guide/apa/ IEEE format: http://pitt.libguides.com/citationhelp/ieee MLA format: http://www.easybib.com/guides/citation-guides/mla-format/ 7/30/2025 33

Writing 7/30/2025 34 Some people never manage to write one 99% perspiration 1% inspiration? If you’re lucky, your thesis will be read by: Your supervisor Your thesis committee

Writing … 7/30/2025 35 This is the hardest part Allocate 2 to 3 times the amount of time to writing as to gathering material preparation for writing your thesis begins as soon as you start your research Learn to draft and revise

Writing … 7/30/2025 36 Keep a research log or series of short 'papers' explaining your work Try to anticipate what you will need to produce as the final written account, and organise your records accordingly Prepare and continually update a table of contents of your thesis Analyse the data thoroughly and think actively about the interpretation of information as you proceed with your research Start to do some writing as your research progresses

Writing … 7/30/2025 37 Prepare an extended outline. Use MS Word “outline” tool List each section and subsection For each section and subsection, write a brief point-form description of the contents . Review with your advisor. Look for unnecessary material? Remove it. missing material? Add it It is much less painful and more time-efficient to make such decisions early, during the outline phase, rather than after you've already done a lot of writing which has to be thrown away.

Writing … 7/30/2025 38 Avoid ornate language, words you don’t really understand (look it up!) Avoid convoluted writing Shed light on your subject: clarity is everything

Writing … 7/30/2025 39 Grammar and Sentence Structure Use active constructions. For example, say “ the operating system starts the device” instead of “the device is started by the operating system” Write in the present tense. Focus on results and not the people/circumstances in which they were obtained Be careful to only draw conclusions that the evidence supports

Thesis ... A reader/reviewer will expect that: you have identified a worthwhile problem or question which has not been previously answered you have solved the problem or answered the question A reader/reviewer will ask: what is the research question? is it a good question? (has it been answered before? is it a useful question to work on?) did the author convince me that the question was adequately answered? has the author made an adequate contribution to knowledge? 7/30/2025 4

Writing … 40 Don’t start with the Introduction or Conclusion Start where you feel comfortable Typically a middle chapter Write outwards Finally Conclusions and end with the Introduction Write everything with your thesis message in mind You’ll discover holes in your research Theorems you haven’t proved Experiments you didn’t run Different problems or parameters Mix writing with more research

Format Specifications 41 Use single-sided, on high quality white A4 paper (201 × 297 mm; 80 grams) font type Times New Roman, font size 12 (except for tables and figures) and justified body of the text should be typed with double spacing. Single-spacing is only permitted in tables, long quotations, footnotes, citation and in the references.

format … 42 Allow at least TWO days just to print and check your thesis - it really does take this long Print a draft copy first and check for errors before printing final copies - get a friend or partner to proof-read the thesis to check for typos Print one chapter at a time - don’t try to merge the thesis into one document and print it as a whole Check each thesis copy to ensure that all the pages are numbered sequentially

Problems with Writing 43 Problem 1: How do I plan? When do I plan? The best way and time is at the beginning: Confirmation, Progress Reports, laboratory books (diaries/journals) are all part of thesis writing

Problems with Writing… 44 Problem 2: I think I have writer’s block work on manageable pieces write down the main points – revisit your theme you don’t have to write it in order accept there are several drafts – get the ideas down complex ideas may not be linear – draw a ‘concept map’ Leave the draft for a few days and come back later with a fresh look

Problems with Writing… 45 Problem 3 : I have trouble showing it is my idea Recommended passive tenses can cloud attributions. Don’t be afraid to play with sentence structure to get the idea across practice expression and interpretation as a team.

Common Problems 46 It’s never possible to cover all issues So you will never finish? It’s sometimes enough to identify the issues Examiners greatly appreciate you identifying limitations Much of your thesis is joint work Identify some work that is yours alone Include a statement at the start of your contributions: “Results from this thesis appear in the following publications. Whilst much of this thesis is joint work with my supervisor, I made significant contributions to Chapters 3-6. In particular, … . “

Common Mistakes 47 Informal text Examiners will jump on imprecision Opinions “.. The main problem in CP is modelling ..” A thesis is an argument! “.. A major bottleneck preventing the uptake of CP is modelling [ Freuder , AAAI-98]

Common Mistakes… 48 Avoid words: bad, good, nice, terrible, stupid Perfect – nothing is !! would seem to show – what matters is the fact Different – different than what? lots of, kind of, just about  vague and colloquial The use of I, you, we – third person Can/may Avoid complex sentences full of long words A thesis should be a simple, convincing argument! Avoid entertainment or humour oke footnote

What do Examiners Do? 49 Typical scan of a thesis Abstract Conclusion Content Listing Figures Major Chapters Introduction, Literature Review Finally, a quick read Then – Questions: Are they answered and/or considered? Is any of it published? Corrections: ..... ..... ..... 7/30/2025

What a Thesis is Not? 7/30/2025 5 What I did in the lab over the last 3 years I first read the background material I then implemented an algorithm I ran some experiments … A thesis is a logical reconstruction Not a historical narrative A brain dump of everything you’ve done

7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 50

Thesis vs. Dissertation Thesis is usually associated with a full research mode degree Dissertation is more common term for the research project required a degree by coursework Western countries use the terms interchangeably 7/30/2025 6

Attributes of Good Thesis It should be contestable, proposing an arguable point with which people could reasonably disagree. A strong thesis is provocative; it takes a stand and justifies the discussion you will present. It tackles a subject that could be adequately covered in the format of the project assigned. It is specific and focused. It clearly asserts your own conclusion based on evidence. 7/30/2025 7

Attributes of Good Thesis… It provides the reader with a map to guide him/her through your work. It anticipates and refutes the counter-arguments It avoids vague language (like "it seems"). It avoids the first person. It should pass the “So What” or “Who Cares” test. Specific topic + Argument = Thesis What you plan to argue + How you plan to argue it = Thesis 7/30/2025 8

Originality & Contribution You must present a thorough review of the existing literature on the subject, and on closely related subjects. Make direct reference to your literature revi ew You must demonstrate that your question has not been previously answered is worth answering 7/30/2025 9

fields.title: RM_Lec_13_14_Research Proposal
fields.extracted_text:
Research Proposal SWE 686 Research Design and Applications for Data and Analysis 13-14 7 Summer 2025 Dr. S M Hasan Mahmud Associate Professor, Department of Software Engineering drhasan.swe@diu.edu.bd

Generalized Form of a RP 7/30/2025 10

Project Title 7/30/2025 11 The title of a research project should be clear and specific. More than ten words are recommended for the title. Acronyms should not be used as the assessment panel might not be familiar with their use The title should indirectly indicate the science/engineering discipline of the research so that the readers can direct the project proposal to those who have relevant experience in the discipline.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 12 While the project outline contains technical detail, the project team needs to convince the project assessment team that the project is worthy of funding. Often the assessment team consists of generalists who do not have specific detailed knowledge of the research topic and the analysis methods proposed in the project outline. Provide a short, clear statement about how the project will benefit their organization and how it fits into the goals and objectives of the research field.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 13 Example: An antenna team aims to design new antennas that will improve the range of small wireless sensors used in a nursing home. The assessment team from the research granting authority might want to know the current and intended application of the wireless sensors and how an increased range will improve the lives of aged people living independently. The project summary should also contain keywords so that the literature in the field can be thoroughly surveyed for competing technologies and research outcomes.

Background 7/30/2025 14 Background information should justify the necessity of your project . Do preliminary studies indicate that your project is necessary and feasible? Literature review: Include title of citation, authors, date, journal and page number Check the citations for accuracy

Background… 7/30/2025 15 Start with a very broad perspective of the main subject area, before gradually narrowing the focus to the central problem under investigation. In doing so, cover the following aspects of your study area: an overview of the main area under study; a historical perspective (development, growth, etc.) pertinent to the study area; philosophical or ideological issues relating to the topic; trends in terms of prevalence, if appropriate; major theories, if any; the main issues, problems and advances in the subject area under study; important theoretical and practical issues relating to the central problem under study; the main findings relating to the core issue(s).

Background… 7/30/2025 16 A presentation of any pertinent data obtained in the investigator’s lab (if any) Displayed in an organized fashion (tables, graphs and other figures) Illustrates the skills and techniques available to the investigator Includes a section of recent publications or manuscripts by the investigator Sec: F /G/I

Problem Statement 7/30/2025 17 Some of the main research questions that you would like to answer through your study should also be raised, and a rationale and relevance for each should be provided. Specifically, this section should: identify the issues that are the basis of your study; specify the various aspects of/perspectives on these issues; identify the main gaps in the existing body of knowledge; raise some of the main research questions that you want to answer through your study; identify what knowledge is available concerning your questions, specifying the differences of opinion in the literature regarding these questions if differences exist; develop a rationale for your study with particular reference to how your study will fill the identified gaps.

Objectives of the Study 7/30/2025 18 The objectives of the study should be clearly stated and specific in nature. Each sub-objective should delineate only one issue. Use action-oriented verbs such as ‘to determine’, ‘to investigate’ and ‘to analyse’ in formulating sub-objectives, which should be numerically listed.

Objectives of the Study: Example 7/30/2025 19 Problem Statement: When will a MEMs based silicon micro-switch fail if it is exposed to the atmosphere? Objectives: To measure the surface contamination of silicon exposed to the atmosphere; To measure the mechanical properties of a silicon beam when subjected to continued use; To model the experimentally derived data using 3D multi-physics .

Content Introduction Research proposal content Project title, project summary and Background Ethical issues Research plan, methodology, timing Budget and justification Research team Research timeline 7/30/2025 2

Measurement Procedures 7/30/2025 20 Justify your choice of research tool, highlighting its strengths and pointing out its weaknesses. Then outline the major segments of your research tool and their relevance to the main objectives of the study. If you are using a standard instrument, briefly discuss the availability of evidence on its reliability and validity. If you adapt or modify it in any way, describe and explain the changes you have made. Ideally, for quantitative studies you should attach a copy of the research instrument to your proposal.

Ethical Issues 7/30/2025 21 All academic institutions are particular about any ethical issues that research may have. To deal with them, all institutions have some form of policy on ethics. You need to be acquainted with your institution’s policy. It is imperative that in your proposal you identify any ethical issues and describe how you propose to deal with them

Problems and Limitations This section should list any problems you think you might encounter concerning, for example, the availability of data, securing permission from the agency/organisation to carry out the study, obtaining the sample, or any other aspect of the study. Problems refer to difficulties relating to logistical details. It is important to be aware of – and communicate – any limitations that could affect the validity of your conclusions and generalisations. 7/30/2025 22

Research Plan, Methodology & Timing Experimental design Innovations Limitations / anticipated difficulties Alternative approaches Time sequence 7/30/2025 23

Research Plan ... Organized sequentially by specific aims stated A detailed description of proposed experiments including: Rationale Design Anticipated results and Alternative strategies Should be planned to methodically address the specific questions stated under specific aims 7/30/2025 24

Analysis of Data In general terms, describe the strategy you intend to use for data analysis. Specify whether the data will be analysed manually or by computer. For computer analysis, identify the program and where appropriate the statistical procedures you plan to perform on the data. For quantitative studies also identify the main variables for cross-tabulation. You need to specify which particular strategy you are proposing for data analysis for your study . 7/30/2025 25

Significance, Outcomes & Innovation Show how the results of your research will fill identified gaps in existing knowledge. Will the results of your research have benefits the country and/or the funding agency? Provide a list of deliverables which will arise from the project. Deliverables are things which can be accessed by others such as reports, software code, presentations, images, etc. Reports, presentations, patents, designs, objects, etc. can be delivered to the granting authority as an indication of progress in the research project. New knowledge is only created when it is publicly available. In some cases, the research team might specify the conferences and journals where their results will be disseminated. 7/30/2025 26

budget 7/30/2025 27 The budget is a very important aspect of the research proposal. The research plan must be a cost effective strategy for the interested partners. Don’t inflate figures An inflated, unjustified budget is likely to result in no funding or reduced funding of the project. Make accurate calculations Direct Costs can be specifically documented, eg : salaries; operating expenses (printing, consumables); travel (mileage, fares, lodging, sustenance); equipment; etc. ..

Budget… Indirect Costs real costs that cannot be easily identified as specific to a particular project, eg : utilities; maintenance of space and equipment; security; computer services; legal services; accounting services; payroll services. 7/30/2025 28

Budget Justification Justify every item. Do not merely restate proposed expenditure. The justification of the budget must match the research methods and analysis outlined in the research proposal. Explain why the project could not proceed/would not be successful without these items 7/30/2025 29

Objectives To understand how to write a research  proposal To understand the structure of research  proposal To understand how to cite literatures To understand how to write citation and  reference 7/30/2025 3

7/30/2025 30

Conflict of Interest When a research team receives funding from a commercial partner, some ethical issues might arise In particular, researchers must ensure that the results are presented in an unbiased manner and that all conflicts of interest are clearly stated in any publications, presentations and reports This can be done in the acknowledgements section of the published paper 7/30/2025 31

Research Team It is rare for a project to involve only one researcher. The project leader (often called the Chief Investigator or Principal Investigator) should take the lead in approaching people who will make a substantial contribution to the project. The project leader is the person who will manage the project in all respects, who will be responsible for the expenditure of the budget, ensure that the milestones are met and the project outcomes are delivered. 7/30/2025 32

Research Team… In higher education for a PhD thesis, the project leader is usually the research student, however, the thesis topic is commonly initiated by the primary academic supervisor. –Commonly students do not control the expenditure of research funds. This is usually the responsibility of the primary supervisor. If the project contains many co-researchers and collaborators: Explain the roles of each participant in the project. Include MoU/ MoA /LOI in the proposal when collaborating with other institution/industry 7/30/2025 33

Research Schedule You must set yourself dates as you need to complete the research within a certain time-frame. List the various operational steps you need to undertake and indicate against each the date by which you aim to complete that task. Remember to keep some time towards the end as a ‘cushion’ for unforeseen circumstances. In a research project, there is a long and unexpected delay in the delivery of a test instrument. Develop a chart. 7/30/2025 34

Gantt Chart 7/30/2025 35

Research Timelines Simple representation of timelines for a project. The crosses represent major time requirements. Q refers to one quarter of one year. Only the major deliverables  should be included. The work titles must be specific (not generic as presented in this illustration). 7/30/2025 36

Research Timelines … 7/30/2025 37

Appendix Include relevant publications/ manuscripts in preparation from your lab Any detailed experimental protocols/methods that are too long to include in the grant In the case of quantitative studies, attach your research instrument. Attach flowchart of your research activities 7/30/2025 38

Reasons for Rejection Absence of innovative ideas or hypothesis Will provide only an incremental advance Not exciting or cutting edge Errors Unclear or incomplete expression of aims Faulty logic or experimental design Less than rigorous presentation 7/30/2025 39

Where we are? 7/30/2025 4

Reasons for Rejection Unrealistic, sloppy or incomplete Resources and facilities not in place PI qualifications/expertise not evident Necessary collaborations not documented 7/30/2025 40

COTENTS OF RESEARCH PROPOSAL 7/30/2025 41

What is Research Proposal? After you have ideas, then you can state the  general problems or general issues of your  “research” interest Elaborate the problems by reviewing  literatures Make sure the problems are worth of research See previous course‐material: how to review  literatures Design steps to solve the problems‐‐ methodology Use all related theories, methods, techniques,  processes, procedures, tools, etc., found in the 7/30/2025 5

RESEARCH PROPOSAL FLOW CHART 7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 6

Introduction A research proposal is an overall plan, scheme, structure and strategy designed to obtain answer to the research question Ensures the reviewers of the validity of the methodology for obtaining answers to your research questions accurately and objectively. Explain to your research supervisor and reviewers the following information about your study : what you are proposing to do; how you plan to find answers to what you are proposing; why you selected the proposed strategies of investigation 7/30/2025 7

7/30/2025 Dr. Afroza Nahar 8

Research Proposal Content A research proposal should contain the following information about your study: an introduction, including a brief literature review, theoretical framework that underpins your study objectives or research questions of your study hypotheses to be tested, if applicable study design that you are proposing to adopt setting for your study research instrument(s) you are planning to use ethical issues involved and how you propose to deal with them data processing procedures problems and limitations of the study proposed time-frame for the project 7/30/2025 9

Research Proposal SWE 686 Research Design and Applications for Data and Analysis 13-14 7 Summer 2025 Dr. S M Hasan Mahmud Associate Professor, Department of Software Engineering drhasan.swe@diu.edu.bd

Generalized Form of a RP 7/30/2025 10

Project Title 7/30/2025 11 The title of a research project should be clear and specific. More than ten words are recommended for the title. Acronyms should not be used as the assessment panel might not be familiar with their use The title should indirectly indicate the science/engineering discipline of the research so that the readers can direct the project proposal to those who have relevant experience in the discipline.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 12 While the project outline contains technical detail, the project team needs to convince the project assessment team that the project is worthy of funding. Often the assessment team consists of generalists who do not have specific detailed knowledge of the research topic and the analysis methods proposed in the project outline. Provide a short, clear statement about how the project will benefit their organization and how it fits into the goals and objectives of the research field.

Project Summary 7/30/2025 13 Example: An antenna team aims to design new antennas that will improve the range of small wireless sensors used in a nursing home. The assessment team from the research granting authority might want to know the current and intended application of the wireless sensors and how an increased range will improve the lives of aged people living independently. The project summary should also contain keywords so that the literature in the field can be thoroughly surveyed for competing technologies and research outcomes.

Background 7/30/2025 14 Background information should justify the necessity of your project . Do preliminary studies indicate that your project is necessary and feasible? Literature review: Include title of citation, authors, date, journal and page number Check the citations for accuracy

Background… 7/30/2025 15 Start with a very broad perspective of the main subject area, before gradually narrowing the focus to the central problem under investigation. In doing so, cover the following aspects of your study area: an overview of the main area under study; a historical perspective (development, growth, etc.) pertinent to the study area; philosophical or ideological issues relating to the topic; trends in terms of prevalence, if appropriate; major theories, if any; the main issues, problems and advances in the subject area under study; important theoretical and practical issues relating to the central problem under study; the main findings relating to the core issue(s).

Background… 7/30/2025 16 A presentation of any pertinent data obtained in the investigator’s lab (if any) Displayed in an organized fashion (tables, graphs and other figures) Illustrates the skills and techniques available to the investigator Includes a section of recent publications or manuscripts by the investigator Sec: F /G/I

Problem Statement 7/30/2025 17 Some of the main research questions that you would like to answer through your study should also be raised, and a rationale and relevance for each should be provided. Specifically, this section should: identify the issues that are the basis of your study; specify the various aspects of/perspectives on these issues; identify the main gaps in the existing body of knowledge; raise some of the main research questions that you want to answer through your study; identify what knowledge is available concerning your questions, specifying the differences of opinion in the literature regarding these questions if differences exist; develop a rationale for your study with particular reference to how your study will fill the identified gaps.

Objectives of the Study 7/30/2025 18 The objectives of the study should be clearly stated and specific in nature. Each sub-objective should delineate only one issue. Use action-oriented verbs such as ‘to determine’, ‘to investigate’ and ‘to analyse’ in formulating sub-objectives, which should be numerically listed.

Objectives of the Study: Example 7/30/2025 19 Problem Statement: When will a MEMs based silicon micro-switch fail if it is exposed to the atmosphere?
